data.content: I can see the project — the express test expects `15.00m` but `CalculateShipping` returns `10.00m`, so that test will fail. Let me start `dotnet watch test` so it re-runs automatically when you save changes:
data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. I can see the express test expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m, so that test will fail. Let me set up `dotnet watch test` for them.

The project uses MSTest with .NET 8.0. I'll start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can iterate.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-a2958ba0a55c41bf808591351977690f && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — failed (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

The watcher is active — every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, it will automatically rebuild and re-run the tests. To fix the failing test, you'd update the `"express"` case from `10.00m` to `15.00m`. Want me to make that fix?
